text: Oh, come on. You knew I was going to have to cover this eventually. No list of self-imposed celebrity meltdowns would be complete without talking about Amber's turd. Possibly the most disastrous series of unforced errors, performative victimhood, bad acting, and overestimating one's own cultural power that Hollywood's ever seen. And that's saying something, believe me. A one-time it girl who seemed to have it all. a growing celebrity profile, juicy film roles in big franchises, brand deals,

and most importantly, a famous husband she could exploit for her own gain. Unfortunately, all those red carpet smiles and elegant dresses were hiding a dark personal life of abuse, lies, manipulation, and an insatiable hunger for power and control. someone who tried to weaponize the media, modern culture, and even the justice system against one of the biggest and most popular movie stars of all time only to have it blow up in her face in the most spectacular

way possible. So, let us examine the not so tragic crash and burn of Amber Herds. Amber was born in 1986 in Austin, Texas, the second of three children. And by all accounts, she had a pretty normal childhood. She rode horses, hunted and fished with her dad, and competed in local beauty pageantss, something she'd disowned in later life as exploitative and horrible. Funny how that works, isn't it? Anyway, like a million other attractive young women with aspirations

for bigger things, Amber made the move to California at 18 to pursue an acting career. The usual round of bit parts and minor roles in TV shows and movies followed, but 2006 would see her first starring role in the slasher movie All the Boys Love Mandy Lane. The film wouldn't get a proper release until years later, but it caused a bit of a stir in film festival circles, and it helped to put Amber Heard on the map. 2008 was when she first started to attract mainstream attention with

supporting roles in Pineapple Express and the martial arts drama Never Back Down. Not exactly an all-time classic, but never mind. Attractive and just talented enough to hold her own against major players. She was now booking bigger roles, but mostly in low-budget slop like Exterminators, The Stepfather, and The Wards. It was also around this time that she came out as gay or bisexual or I don't know, something. Even Amber herself seemed to be pretty vague about the whole thing, saying, "I

don't label myself one way or the other." Well, that's helpful. Anyway, the point is she dated female photographer Taza Van Ree for a couple of years and this is where the first warning signs appeared when Amber was arrested at an airport in Washington for assault against Van Ree. She would later claim that she was wrongfully accused and the officer who arrested her was homophobic, which is kind of interesting because the officer in question was a lesbian. Oh well, whatever. The incident was

quickly swept under the rug, as such things often are, and Amber was able to tackle the bigger problem of how to make the leap from trashy horror movies to mainstream success. What she really needed was something or someone to take her career to the next level.

Enter Johnny Depp. The two of them met on the set of The Rum Diary and started dating soon afterwards. Yeah, he was 23 years her senior. But what the [ __ ] when you're a struggling young actress trying to make a dent in the industry and Captain Jack Sparrow shows an interest in you. You thank your lucky stars and milk that [ __ ] for all it's worth. The two were married by 2015 which funnily enough seemed to coincide with a sudden upswing in Amber's career in public

image. Now she was booking roles in major franchise vehicles like Justice League and Aquaman. She was on magazine covers and commercials. She and Johnny were regulars at premieres and film festivals together. But behind the scenes, things were unraveling fast. Her marriage to Depp had been stormy and volatile for years, and by 2016, Amber filed for divorce and a restraining order, accusing him of physical and emotional abuse. The two eventually reached a settlement that landed Amber a

hefty sum of $7 million, which she pledged to donate entirely to charity. Golly G whiz, that was nice of you, Amber. Keep it in mind though because it's going to be important shortly. For now at least though, Amber was still on top of the world. She'd managed to paint herself as a brave victim speaking up against a powerful and abusive man. She'd landed a neat little payout that had patted her bank balance and gotten the entire entertainment media complex to back her up. Because of course they

would. It was 2017. While Johnny found himself blacklisted and dropped from major film projects, Amber's career was going from strength to strength. She'd co-starred in her first billiondoll movie, although to this day, I've got no [ __ ] idea how that happened. And she was poised to make the leak to the Hollywood A-list. Yeah, she wasn't really a movie star, but she had the looks and the glamour and the momentum to at least create the image of one. All she had to do now was shut the [ __ ] up,
maintain a dignified silence about her divorce, and enjoy the fruits of her success. No no no.

[Music] Yeah. So, that didn't happen. For reasons best known only to herself, Amber decided to stir up controversy all over again by writing a 2018 op-ed for the Washington Post, where she once again tried to paint herself as a helpless victim, getting raked over the coals for speaking up about the abuse she'd suffered. What are you, [ __ ] She'd already won both the legal and the PR victory, but she just couldn't help herself. It seems this op-ed would turn

out to be a bridge too far for Amber. To say that Johnny Depp was tired of her [ __ ] would be the understatement of the decade. And before you could say, "You might have overplayed your hand here, love." He'd launched a major defamation lawsuit against her, accusing her not just of lying and falsifying police reports, but of being the real abuser in the relationship. Claims and counter claims were flying around like angry hornets, and the atmosphere was explosive when the case finally went to

trial in 2022. Amber strode into the courtroom, expecting another easy victory. After all, she'd won against him before. The media were still firmly on her side, and she was all set to paint herself as the brave young woman standing up to the patriarchy. Unfortunately for her, things were different this time. Not only had Johnny brought the receipts this time, but Amber soon became caught up in the very web of lies that she'd spent so long spinning. Like, remember that $7 million

from the divorce settlement that she promised to donate to charity because it wasn't about the money for me? Shite. Oh, yes. I'll shite. Yeah. So, that never happened. And it turns out she'd kept every penny for herself. Oh, man. Oh, God. Oh, man. Oh my goodness, who could have predicted it? The evidence of the years of abuse she'd inflicted on Depp was mounting up, too. A recorded phone conversation showed her gloating about how she could make up anything she wanted and everyone would

believe her because she was a woman. Then there was the stuff that was just downright disturbing and weird, like Amber admitting she'd hit him, but claiming that it was okay because it was a slap instead of a punch. Yeah, imagine if a guy had said something like that. Or taking a [ __ ] on Johnny's bed out of pure spite and revenge. [Music] And in that moment, dear viewer, a million internet memes were born. She was still doing her level best to paint herself as a brave victim speaking truth

to power. The problem was that nobody was buying it anymore. Her testimonies came off as forced, rehearsed, and unconvincing, riddled with more contradictions and inconsistencies than a Zack Snider script, and people were beginning to see her for what she actually was. a not particularly talented actress playing the role of a victim instead of actually being one. Now, for the record, I certainly don't think that Johnny Depp was some innocent, blameless saint in all of this

[ __ ] either. The man clearly has his own demons that he's been wrestling with for most of his life and a long list of well publicized meltdowns. I've got no doubt they both brought toxic behavior into the marriage, but the difference is that Depp was always honest about his personal failings. Amber was not. More than that though, people actually liked Johnny Depp. He was popular. He'd been around forever, and he'd built up the kind of cultural goodwill that Amber

could never hope to equal. The more the trial dragged on, the more public opinion swung away from Amber and towards Johnny. And by the time the verdict was read out on June 1st, it was pretty obvious how things were going to go. Johnny was awarded a total of 15 million in damages. But for Amber, the reputational damage was way worse. film roles dried up overnight. Her part in the upcoming Aquaman sequel was shaved down to the briefest of cameos, and she was abandoned by almost everyone who'd

supported her. There's only one thing Hollywood producers hate more than difficult actors, and that's liabilities. The one-time darling of Hollywood, the brave activist, and the rising movie star, had become so toxic and radioactive that nobody in their right mind would go near her. It wasn't just a career meltdown. It was a full-scale career obliteration. Johnny Depp had taken off and nuked Amber's entire sight from orbit because at the end of the day, it's the only way to be

sure. And who can blame him really? The [ __ ] she pulled probably took 5 years off his career and cost him tens of millions of dollars in lost earnings. It was the vindication and the redemption arc that everyone had been waiting for. And by all accounts, Johnny Depp has been rehabilitated by Hollywood. And there he is now back on the red carpets in the film festivals. He's even in talks to star in another Pirates of the Caribbean movie because let's be honest

here, Johnny Depp's Jack Sparrow is Pirates of the Caribbean. Sorry, Margot, but when it comes to pirates movies, you ain't it, darling. And what's of Amber, you might ask? Well, in the wake of the verdict, she fled to Europe under a new identity. And for the past couple of years, she's been working on the next chapter of her life, whoever the [ __ ] that means. Considering she hasn't been in anything since 2023, and there's no plans for any upcoming projects, I

suspect her next chapter is going to consist mostly of vacation time and reflecting on the sheer enormity of her [ __ ] For a few years, Amber Heard really did have it all, only to let it slip through her fingers. She tried to play the fame game in the worst way possible, weaponizing performative victimhood and a culture of believe every single accusation, even without a shred of evidence, for her own personal gain. And when she was called out for her [ __ ] she didn't have the acting

skills, the likability, or the sheer popularity to lie her way out of it. Her crash and burn might be one of the most spectacular of all time. And let's be honest here, one of the most welld deserved. Anyway, that's all I've got for today. Go away now.
